title: The importance of Shinjis Cassette Player
display_title: The importance of Shinjis Cassette Player
wikitext:
[]: The importance of Shinji's Cassette Player             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Thu Jun 17, 2010 10:12 pm Ok I am new to this site but I also post on other Eva sites as well as discuss Eva frequently with my anime club... I dont know if this has been asked yet here on this site but here it goes... Does any one have any ideas about why Shinji's cassette player is so important. It is consistently shown to being playing tracks 26 and 25 and i personally have not many ideas about what this significance is... If you have any thoughts or maybe u know why please let me know

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Jun 17, 2010 10:18 pm See HERE FWIW, I always found the choice of tracks 25/26 quite interesting since those are the episodes NGE ends on. A subtle allusion to Instrumentality? Maybe.

[]:             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Thu Jun 17, 2010 10:24 pm Yes i too thought that because those are the ending episodes of NGE that it would be something dealing with what happens at the end. Still there is the one episode which i cannot remeber which one, ill look it up in a bit and repost here, that the cassette does go into a full rewind and starts back at zero.. bringing in a whole nother thought process

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jun 18, 2010 6:58 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: FWIW, I always found the choice of tracks 25/26 quite interesting since those are the episodes NGE ends on. A subtle allusion to Instrumentality? Maybe.

[]:             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Fri Jun 18, 2010 6:48 pm yes tahts the one...wonder if that is supposed to have any meaning to it as well..

[]:             Quote Post by Conzy91 » Thu Jul 01, 2010 9:07 am I always thought it was a way to convey Shinji's need to escape from the world. He uses it as a tool for locking people out. This seemed one of the most straightforward themes of running away in evangelion to me (along with the symbolism of the train) As with everything in the series it may have more than one meaning but this is what I related to because I do it all the time as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Jul 01, 2010 10:08 am I don't know if you've noticed but when Shinji's SDAT breaks it shows track 27: I don't want to start making futile inferences but this scared me a little.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Jul 01, 2010 12:58 pm It's been noticed, discussed to death, and doesn't belong in this thread anyways, what with Track 27 being Rebuild and all.
